title: gypsophila perfoliata subsp. ilerdensis
intro: Gypsophila perfoliata subsp. ilerdensis, commonly known as Ilerdense baby's breath, is a plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The medicinal properties of Gypsophila perfoliata subsp. ilerdensis include its ability to act as a diuretic, reducing fluid retention and alleviating conditions such as edema and hypertension. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The herbal preparations of Gypsophila perfoliata subsp. ilerdensis can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, although its use should be consulted with a medical professional.
